back_translation: These are the names of the sons of Israel who came into Egypt with Jacob, each one with his family.
Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah.
Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin.
Dan and Naphtali; Gad and Asher.
All those who came out of Jacob's loins were seventy persons; Joseph was already in Egypt.
Afterward, Joseph died, along with all his brothers and all that generation.
But the sons of Israel were fruitful, swarmed, multiplied, and grew exceedingly mighty; the land was filled with them.
Afterward, a new king rose to rule over Egypt; he did not know Joseph.
He said to his people, 'Behold, the people of the sons of Israel are numerous and stronger than us.'
Come, let us deal cunningly with them, lest they multiply further; and when war breaks out, they will join our enemies, fight against us, and depart from the land.
Therefore the Egyptians appointed overseers of forced labor over them, to oppress them with heavy burdens. They built store cities for Pharaoh — Pithom and Rameses.
But the more they oppressed them, the more they multiplied and spread; so the Egyptians came to dread the sons of Israel.
The Egyptians forced the sons of Israel to work cruelly.
They made their lives bitter with hard labor — with mortar and with bricks, and with every kind of work in the fields; every labor they imposed upon them was cruel.
At that time the king of Egypt said to the Hebrew midwives — one named Shiphrah and the other named Puah —
He said, 'When you assist the Hebrew women in childbirth and see them on the birthstool, if it is a son, kill him; but if it is a daughter, let her live.'
But the midwives feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them; they let the boys live.
So the king of Egypt summoned the midwives and said to them, 'Why have you done this — letting the boys live?'
The midwives told Pharaoh, 'The Hebrew women are not like the Egyptian women; they are vigorous, and before the midwife reaches them, they have given birth.'
So God was good to the midwives, and the people multiplied and became very mighty.
Because the midwives feared God, He gave them families.
Then Pharaoh commanded all his people, saying, 'Throw every son who is born into the Nile, but let every daughter live.'
